texto: It is natural that people should differ most, and most violently, about the unknowable . . . There is all the room in the world for divergence of opinion about something that, so far as we can realistically perceive, does not exist.
explicacao: Orientação direta de E. Haldeman-Julius sobre temas ligados a reflexao. A afirmação “It is natural that people should differ most, and most violently, about the unknowable . . . Ther…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a reflexao, differ, divergence, útil em momentos de divergence e existence.

frase_original: It is natural that people should differ most, and most violently, about the unknowable . . . There is all the room in the world for divergence of opinion about something that, so far as we can realistically perceive, does not exist.

texto: We advocate the atheistic philosophy because it is the only clear, consistent position which seems possible to us. As atheists, we simply deny the assumptions of theism; we declare that the God idea, in all its features, is unreasonable and unprovable; we add, more vitally, that the God idea is an interference with the interests of human happiness and progress. We oppose religion not merely as a set of theological ideas; but we must also oppose religion as a political, social and moral influence detrimental to the welfare of humanity.
explicacao: Orientação direta de E. Haldeman-Julius sobre temas ligados a fe. A afirmação “We advocate the atheistic philosophy because it is the only clear, consistent position which seem…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a fe, atheism, atheists, útil em momentos de atheists e detrimental.

frase_original: We advocate the atheistic philosophy because it is the only clear, consistent position which seems possible to us. As atheists, we simply deny the assumptions of theism; we declare that the God idea, in all its features, is unreasonable and unprovable; we add, more vitally, that the God idea is an interference with the interests of human happiness and progress. We oppose religion not merely as a set of theological ideas; but we must also oppose religion as a political, social and moral influence detrimental to the welfare of humanity.
